title: Home | Optimized AI Conference | Speakers | Workshops

headings.text: Join us for one or both days

body_text.text: MARCH 30th & 31st, 2026 | ATL, GA

An affordable, hands-on AI conference designed for students, professionals, and executives alike. Gain real skills, practical strategies, and tools you can use immediately, without the high conference price tag.
Senior managers and above are eligible to apply for a complimentary ticket -- email team@oaiconference.com with your LinkedIn profile to request access.
Teams of 3+ can attend with 30% off using code GROUP30.
Get 25% off when you repost a conference update on LinkedIn or X. Email a screenshot to team@oaiconference.com, and we'll send you your discount code.

Volunteers play a key role in making the conference run smoothly. Open to students and professionals, volunteers receive access to talks and workshops after their shift. Register for an early bird ticket with code VOL-2026 -- limited tickets available.
Hands-On Technical Workshops: Go beyond the theory with deep-dive sessions led by industry pioneers. Master the full stack of agentic AI -- from GPU-level inference to multi-agent orchestration.
Workshops take place on the afternoon of Day 2 at 1:00 PM on March 31.
Anton Alexander - Sr. GenAI Specialist for NVIDIA - AWS. Mastering Agentic Coding and GPUs is a hands-on, 2-hour workshop focused on building, deploying, and scaling production-ready agentic systems.

body_text.section: Workshop: Multi Agent Architecture using ADK
body_text.text: Hamza Farooq - CEO - Traversaal AI (Ex-Google). Build collaborative multi-agent systems using Google's A2A protocol, MCP, and the Google Agent Development Kit (ADK).

body_text.section: Workshop: Optimizing Retrieval for Agentic Systems
body_text.text: Trey Grainger - Founder & CEO, Searchkernel. Learn core techniques and design patterns behind modern AI-powered retrieval, including agentic search with tool calls and relevance feedback loops.
Zeyuan (Faradawn) Yang - Technical Marketing Engineer - NVIDIA. Optimize GPU performance and reduce operational costs when training and serving large language models.

Context Engineering with Redis and LangChain. Design scalable AI agents by mastering context engineering -- the discipline of structuring memory, retrieval, and reasoning workflows so LLMs behave reliably in production.
Engineering Security, Compliance, and Governance from Ground Up. Agentic AI systems are moving from experimentation to production, autonomously browsing the web, executing code, and calling APIs.
NVIDIA - Carter Abdallah - DevTech Engineer. Explore how agentic CLI tools go beyond coding, how vibe insights reduce the distance between data and action.

body_text.section: Workshop: Production Ready Agentic AI Systems
body_text.text: Jonathan Vogel - Developer Advocate - AWS. Learn to build AI agents for real-world apps using Strands SDK.
Brian Turcotte - Developer Relations - KiloCode. Deploy and run a fully-managed OpenClaw agent with KiloClaw.
Hamza Farooq - Founder - Traversaal.ai/ex-Google. Comprehensive understanding of designing and building AI agents from the ground up.
Trey Grainger - Author - AI-Powered Search. RAG systems tuning involves deep knowledge of both vector and traditional keyword search.
Mark Moyou, PhD - Sr. Data Scientist - NVIDIA. Training and deploying large DNNs in NLP, vision, and speech AI.
Head of AI - Members (Kamelia Aryafar)
SVP ML/AI - Chief Scientist (Mike Tamir)
AI Developer Relations Lead (Paige Bailey)
DevTech Engineer (Carter Abdallah)

02:30 - 02:35 PM: Closing
$100 - Access to all talks, Light Refreshments
$250 - Limited early access at an extreme discount. Includes workshops and talks (no meals).
$400 - Two days of conference talks, including keynotes and breakout sessions, with meals included -- plus half-day hands-on workshops.
Free - Volunteer during the event and get access to all talks (excluding workshops) after your shift. No meals included.

Paige Bailey, AI Developer Relations Lead: 'Last September, I had the privilege of attending and speaking at the Optimized AI Conference. It was a valuable experience to exchange cutting-edge ideas with industry leaders.'

Sebastian Raschka, Staff Research Engineer - Lightning AI: 'If you're looking for a generative AI conference that feels like home, you'd be hard-pressed to find a more welcoming event than Optimized AI in Atlanta!'
Mike Tamir, PhD, Distinguished Scientist: 'The Optimized AI conference was a fantastic event where I had the opportunity to share innovative ideas as a speaker and connect with industry leaders.'
Principal AI Engineer - Arcade AI: 'This conference has been something I've looked forward to over the years as a venue to share and discuss practical applications.'
Jaya Kawale, VP of Engineering: 'Every time I'm glad I did so. The quality and density of content outshines many conferences on the same tier.'
Ugur Kursuncu, Assistant Professor - Georgia State University: 'A great place to share practical, bleeding-edge ideas with practitioners and leaders.'
Take advantage of the plethora of learning resources (free and paid) that we have curated to help you accelerate your career.
Tutorials, Podcasts, Courses, Career Resources/Jobs

As practitioners, we recruit speakers from top companies to bring you the latest industry best practices.
Day 1: Conference Talks. Day 2: Conference Talks + Workshops.
Cobb Galleria Center. Hotels Nearby.

metadata.description: Tickets under $250 -- Optimized AI Conference 2026 is a practitioner-focused AI event in Atlanta, March 30-31, 2026, featuring 50+ talks and workshops from experts at Google, Microsoft, NVIDIA, Walmart, Netflix, AWS, Meta, and more. Learn how to build, deploy, and scale agentic systems, multi-agent architectures, and production AI, with deep coverage of optimization, evals, observability, and AI strategy.
